title: epipactis atropurpurea subvar. lutescens
intro: Epipactis atropurpurea subvar. lutescens is a type of orchid, also known as the purple helleborine, a perennial flowering plant that belongs to the family Orchidaceae. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, such as relieving respiratory issues, reducing inflammation, and supporting the immune system. In terms of herbal preparations, Epipactis atropurpurea subvar. lutescens is typically dried and used in teas, infusions, or tinctures, although more research is needed to fully understand its potential uses and effects.
